title: Nexus PG Metadata JSONB Schema Contract
description: Defines allowed keys, types, and validation rules for metadata JSONB columns across all PG tables. Enforced at db-write.sh boundary. Version 1.0 — TKT-0299 (Atlas AC1-R2).
author: Atlas + Yoda
scope: All metadata JSONB columns in ainchors_nexus PG database

state_tickets.description: Core ticket registry. Metadata holds extended/evolving fields beyond the fixed schema columns.

owner.description: Agent or user assigned as owner

parent.description: Parent ticket ID (e.g. TKT-0295 for child tickets)

requester.description: Who requested the ticket (Ken, Angie, Yoda, etc.)

resolution.description: Resolution notes when ticket is closed

closedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when ticket was closed

validation.note: Unknown keys are allowed (flexible schema-on-read pattern). They are logged but not rejected. At P2 multi-client, consider tightening to rejectUnknownKeys.

state_latency.description: Cron job latency tracking. Metadata holds source attribution.

source.description: Source script that recorded this latency entry

epoch.description: Unix epoch timestamp of the tracked event

validation.note: state_latency is write-only from latency-tracker.sh. Tight validation — unknown keys rejected.

changelog.description: Change log entries (CHG records). Metadata holds migration provenance.

validation.note: changelog metadata is migration-provenance only. Unknown keys rejected.

governance.reviewSchedule: Quarterly (with EA review per A4 guardrail)
governance.changeProcess: Schema changes require Atlas review + CHG record. Additive changes (new keys) are low-risk. Removal or type changes require migration plan.
